2604.16744.title: Evaluating Adaptive Personalization of Educational Readings with Simulated Learners
2604.16744.paper_summary: We present a framework for evaluating adaptive personalization of educational reading materials with theory-grounded simulated learners. The system builds a learning-objective and knowledge-component ontology from open textbooks, curates it in a browser-based Ontology Atlas, labels textbook chunks with ontology entities, and generates aligned reading-assessment pairs. Simulated readers learn from passages through a Construction-Integration-inspired memory model with DIME-style reader factors, KREC-style misconception revision, and an open New Dale-Chall readability signal. Answers are produced by score-based option selection over the learner's explicit memory state, while BKT drives adaptation. Across three sampled subject ontologies and matched cohorts of 50 simulated learners per condition, adaptive reading significantly improved outcomes in computer science, yielded smaller positive but inconclusive gains in inorganic chemistry, and was neutral to slightly negative in general biology.
2604.16744.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個框架，用於評估教育閱讀材料的自適應個性化，並以理論為基礎的模擬學習者進行評估。該系統從開放教科書中構建學習目標和知識組件本體，並在基於瀏覽器的本體圖譜中進行整理，將教科書的片段標記為本體實體，並生成對應的閱讀評估配對。模擬讀者通過一種受建構-整合啟發的記憶模型學習段落，該模型結合了DIME風格的讀者因素、KREC風格的誤解修正以及開放的New Dale-Chall可讀性信號。答案是通過對學習者的顯式記憶狀態進行基於分數的選項選擇來產生的，而BKT則驅動適應。在三個抽樣的學科本體和每個條件下50名模擬學習者的匹配隊列中，自適應閱讀在計算機科學方面顯著改善了結果，在無機化學中產生了較小的正向但不確定的增益，而在普通生物學中則是中立到略微負面。
2604.16744.author: Ryan T. Woo et.al.
2604.16744.authors: Ryan T. Woo, Anmol Rao, Aryan Keluskar, Yinong Chen
